---
title: "Shyleaf (Aeschynomene americana)"
slug: "aeschynomene-americana"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/355087663/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Shyleaf"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Fabales"
tax_family: "Fabaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Fabales", "Fabaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/355087663/large.jpg" alt="Shyleaf" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/larvalfishguy">
larvalfishguy</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/201181472">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Fabales
-   **Family:** Fabaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=63705)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in moist, disturbed sites.

## Natural history notes

A leguminous plant characterized by its sensitive, pinnate leaves that fold when touched and its small yellow to orange flowers. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as ditches, marsh edges, and wet fields. It grows during the warmer months and produces segmented seed pods, while also contributing to soil fertility through nitrogen fixation.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/63705-Aeschynomene-americana)
-   [Local profile](https://mrec.ifas.ufl.edu/resources/weedsbyflowercolor/pinkorblue/aeschynomeneamericana/)
